content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 9, 1993

Beverly Korbin
18100 Chardon Circle
Encino, California 91316

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-045

Dear Ms. Korbin:

You have requested advice on behalf of Los Angeles Unified
School District Board Member Roberta Weintraub regarding the
campaign and personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act"). I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation
on February 8, 1993, wherein we discussed your request for advice
and the further questions you faxed as an addendum to your letter.
In response to your questions, I informed you as follows:

Question 1: Ms. Weintraub may transfer the funds in her
campaign account to a new account for a future election. However,
please note that if she plans to take this action, she must do so
before the funds in her current account become "surplus." Under
Government Code Section 89519, campaign funds become "surplus"
when the candidate leaves office, or at the end of the
postelection reporting period following the defeat of a candidate
for elective office, whichever occurs last. As we discussed,
Section 89519 does not permit the transfer of campaign funds to a
new account for a future election once the funds have become
"surplus."

I suggested that you contact our Technical Assistance
Division for information regarding the proper procedures to open a
new campaign account (Sections 85200 and 85201). If Ms. Weintraub
does not seek a future candidacy and does not open a new campaign
account, she may contribute the funds to the Van Nuys High/Valley
Presbyterian Medical Magnet Program Scholarship Fund, which you
stated meets the criteria in Government Code Section 89519(c).

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-93-045
Page 2


Question II: Ms. Weintraub may offer a pro-rata return of
contributions to contributors and return those funds to
contributors who request such a return.

Question III: Before she leaves office, Ms. Weintraub may
transfer the funds in her account to the candidate who is running
for her seat on the Los Angeles Board of Education. However, once
the funds have become "surplus," as discussed above, such a
transfer would be prohibited. You stated that the local
contribution limits do not pertain to the school board election.

I trust this answers your questions.

Sincerely,


Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division


JM:JS:dl
